judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with solid grounding; Report 2 additionally catches the unsupported "production data" caption issue (bonus not required by expectations), giving it a slight edge in thoroughness.

report_1.evidence: "Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default." `clinic-portal/app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` ... "Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF." `clinic-portal/app/routers/visits.py` implements only `GET /visits` returning an empty list `[]`, with a trailing comment `# TODO: pdf export`. There is no PDF generation code anywhere in the repo... the claim must be removed

report_2.evidence: "Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF." — False as of this commit. `app/routers/visits.py` (Action 11) only returns an empty list from `/visits` and contains a bare `# TODO: pdf export` comment... "Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default." — False. `app/security/config.py` (Action 12) sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`

report_1.evidence: `captures.json` records that `after_scheduling.png` was actually captured from route `/billing/invoices`, not `/schedule`... `before_scheduling.png` renders the text "SCHEDULING CALENDAR", while `after_scheduling.png` renders "BILLING / INVOICES" — a completely unrelated screen.

report_2.evidence: `before_scheduling.png` shows a screen reading "SCHEDULING CALENDAR," but `after_scheduling.png` shows a screen reading "BILLING / INVOICES"... `after_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/billing/invoices`, while `before_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/schedule`

report_1.evidence: the document embeds `![after](img/after_export.png)`, but no such file exists in `clinic-portal/docs/release/img/`... `unused_capture_07.png` exists in the `img/` directory (I opened it — it renders "PATIENT LIST") but is not referenced anywhere in `highlights-v4.md`

report_2.evidence: **`after_export.png` is missing** — it would render as a broken image in the email... There is also one file on disk, `unused_capture_07.png` (shows "PATIENT LIST," Action 6), that the document never references

report_1.evidence: Yes: the trailing line **"Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** This is clearly an internal drafting note, not customer-facing copy... It should be stripped before send

report_2.evidence: **The trailing line "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** This is internal drafting metadata, not customer-facing content... It should be stripped before sending regardless of other fixes.
